planned_changes.description: Create calculation_log.csv containing row-by-row arithmetic for all reported metrics. Columns: metric_name, formula, operands_with_packet_citations, numeric_result.

Create decision_table.csv mapping inputs and threshold to recommended actions. Columns: TP, FP, TN, FN, defect_prior(optional), threshold_p_defect, weighted_cost_accept, weighted_cost_reject, recommended_action.

Correct confusion-matrix table labels in porosity_QC_report.md so FP = true_defect predicted_good and FN = true_good predicted_reject, and add an explicit legend linking table cells to variable names used in formulas.

Add a numeric sensitivity analysis section: compute weighted_misclassification_cost and false-accept rate under +/-5% perturbations to defect counts and/or defect-prior; summarize impact on the recommendation.

If available, attach calibration artifacts (score-to-probability mapping) or a threshold sweep (ROC/PR or table of candidate thresholds). If unavailable, add an explicit note and the method to compute the calibrated cutoff when those artifacts are produced.
Update ActualOutputs manifest to include the two new machine-readable files and reference them in the report; run deterministic precheck to confirm artifact-contract completeness.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The submitted porosity_QC_report.md contains correct numeric calculations (precision, recall, defect-escape/false-accept rate, and cost-threshold p < 1/6) and clear validation checks, but it is missing required machine-readable artifacts (calculation_log.csv and decision_table.csv), contains a confusion-matrix label inconsistency (FP/FN labels swapped in the table vs. the formulas), and lacks a quantified sensitivity analysis. These issues justify partial credit despite the arithmetic being auditable and correct.
